description_long: This is a stylized vulture with a prominent, bare pink head and neck, and a large, hooked beak. Its wings are outstretched, displaying dark brown primary feathers with lighter, almost beige, secondary feathers and body plumage. The bird's body is robust, with a hunched posture and a ruff of lighter feathers around the neck, typical of scavenger birds. The tail is short and wedge-shaped, with a mix of dark and light feathers. The legs are short and mostly obscured by the body feathers, but appear to be a muted gray or brown. The overall appearance is that of a large, carrion-eating bird commonly seen in open urban or park environments.
description: A stylized vulture with a bare pink head, hooked beak, and outstretched dark brown wings, featuring a ruff of lighter neck feathers and a wedge-shaped tail.
description_view_0: Front view showing the vulture's open wings, bare pink head, hooked beak, and prominent neck ruff.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated neck, curved beak, and layered feather structure along the body and wings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full span of the dark wings, wedge-shaped tail, and the back of the head and neck.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same elongated neck and beak, with visible feather layering and body contour.
attribute_plumage_pattern: Solid dark brown wings with lighter beige body and neck ruff, bare pink head and neck
